text: The patient is positioned in a Trendelenburg position with the left side elevated. The DaVinci XI robot is brought in, aligning its crosshairs with the camera port.
The operator adjusts the vertical height of the robotic boom by pressing the port clutch button for three seconds, allowing the boom to raise or lower to the desired height.
The robotic arms are docked to the da Vinci surgical system, aligning with the ports on the patient positioned in Trendelenburg.
The surgeon aligns the trajectory of the camera port with the center of the dissection area, adjusting the flex joints to ensure the topmost joint is parallel to the camera port arm.
The camera shows the patient clearance joints of the robotic system moving downward, positioned as low as possible without contacting the patient.
The surgeon clears the nozzles of the robotic ports to prevent them from rubbing against the patient during the operation.
